title: Unit 1 – Check-out, Station 3 & Story
description: Check-out, Station 3, Story – Kunst, Gefühle, Alltag

vocab.ex: Whose trainers smell so bad? Yuk!
Why don't you have a password? You're crazy!
It wasn't very polite for a workshop participant to leave their dirty underwear behind.
She's so good on a skateboard, you should see what she does at the skatepark!
Shakespeare grew up in Stratford-upon-Avon and later moved to London.
text – story – song – rhyme – poem
David has a brother whose name is Mark.
Lauren and Rishi don't get on very well.
I always get bored when I read love stories.
The former owner of our house gave us a really cool sofa.
This is our own house. We own it.
I'm looking forward to our holiday in Croatia!
Rishi, Beca and the others are participants in the workshop.
I don't know if I could be a baker, they get up so early in the morning!
He thinks he'll be a singer, but have you seen his videos on social media?
Have you heard about that new phone? They advertise it everywhere.

vocab.de: Plogging (Kombination aus Joggen und Mülleinsammeln als Sportart)
vocab.ex: We should start a plogging group and do something for our park.
Oh my God, this shopping centre is too full, get me out of here!
Where's the excitement at this workshop?
There's a great view from the top of our council tower.
13 isn't a lucky number, so I wouldn't want to live on the 13th floor!
Everybody is scared of that old house, nobody dares to go in.

vocab.de: Sie konnte den Blick nicht von ihm abwenden.

vocab.ex: My day in London was a nightmare! Somebody stole my bag.
Do we really have to read this book, Mrs Lewis? No discussions, Amy. Just read it.
I need to catch the 1:15 train.
What's the distance from Chelsea to Buckingham Palace?
Rishi, you really have a posh accent.
I don't know a word of French, so obviously I'm a bit nervous about the trip to Paris.
That man just pushed me so he could get on the Tube first, how rude!
The train has just passed a bridge.
Good friends stand up for each other.
An everyday situation is the kind of thing that happens every day.
Are you sad? What can I do to cheer you up?
I don't know if I can enjoy myself at this party, everybody is staring at me.
He reached for the last piece of cake, but his sister was faster.
Rishi discovered too late that his phone was gone.
Lauren didn't want to phone her mum.
Who's the new bloke in our street?
You want people to respect your privacy, so it's only fair that you respect theirs.
Rishi and Lauren tricked Jess, but not Beca and Conor.

vocab.de: Aber vielleicht hätten wir es tun sollen.

vocab.ex: I don't know if a weekend workshop is for me. There isn't enough freedom!
What are the dangers of social media?
Some people say, 'The camera never lies.' But you can change photos and trick people too.
I don't like writing reports because they're all about facts. But I love writing lyrical texts.
Being too direct in conversations is not always a good thing.
